title: Ethereum Protocol Studies 2026 Expands with New Learning Tracks
summary: Ethereum Protocol Studies (EPS) is back for 2026 with new content tracks and a self-paced learning platform. The program starts on February 23rd and aims to educate developers and enthusiasts about Ethereum's core technologies.
body:
Ethereum Protocol Studies (EPS) is returning for 2026 with an expanded curriculum and new learning opportunities. The program, which first launched as a 10-week study group, now includes new content tracks in cryptography, lean consensus, and zkEVM. Additionally, a new self-paced learning platform has been introduced to make the material more accessible to a broader audience. The program kicks off on February 23rd, offering a structured way for developers and enthusiasts to deepen their understanding of Ethereum's core technologies.

This expansion means that more people can learn about the intricate workings of Ethereum without needing a deep technical background. The new self-paced platform allows participants to learn at their own speed, making it easier for those with busy schedules to get involved. The addition of tracks like cryptography and zkEVM (a type of technology that enhances privacy and scalability on Ethereum) means that participants can focus on areas that interest them the most.

For everyday users, this means better access to high-quality educational resources about Ethereum. Whether you're a developer looking to build on Ethereum or an enthusiast wanting to understand how the network works, EPS 2026 offers a structured way to learn. The self-paced nature of the platform ensures that you can fit learning into your schedule, making it more accessible than ever.

If you're interested in diving deeper into Ethereum's technology, keep an eye out for the launch of the self-paced learning platform. You can visit epf.wiki to get started and explore the different tracks offered. This is a great opportunity to expand your knowledge and engage more deeply with the Ethereum community.
